TITLE: Senior Full Stack Developer
SUMMARY: Experienced in designing and developing enterprise full-stack applications using Java, Spring Boot, React.js, and modern web technologies.
Strong expertise in building scalable backend services and microservices using Spring Boot, REST APIs, and distributed system design principles.
Proficient in developing responsive and dynamic user interfaces with React.js, JavaScript, HTML, and CSS for enterprise applications.
Skilled in designing and implementing RESTful and GraphQL APIs for secure and efficient system integration.
Hands-on experience supporting event-driven application architectures using Apache Kafka for asynchronous processing and service communication.
Experienced in working with Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL data modeling, partition design, scalability, and performance-oriented access patterns.
Proficient in backend-heavy full-stack development with Java as the primary backend technology while collaborating across Node.js and frontend layers where needed.
Strong understanding of microservices architecture, service decomposition, API orchestration, and resilient distributed system behavior.
Experienced in supporting mobile-enabled platforms through API-driven integrations for React Native, Flutter, or native mobile applications.
Skilled in containerization and deployment support using Docker, Kubernetes, cloud platforms, and enterprise CI/CD workflows.
Hands-on experience with Git-based development, code reviews, testing practices, debugging, and production issue support.
Experienced in Agile collaboration with cross-functional teams including product owners, QA engineers, architects, and release stakeholders.
Strong ability to modernize enterprise applications while balancing scalability, performance, maintainability, and operational support needs.
Proficient in implementing secure, reusable, and maintainable service contracts across frontend, backend, mobile, and integration components.
Experienced in delivering high-quality applications aligned with enterprise deployment practices, release controls, and change management expectations.
